title: agrimonia pubescens var. microcarpa
intro: Agrimonia pubescens var. microcarpa, also known as small-fruited agrimony, is a plant species within the Rosaceae family, characterized by its small white flowers and hairy leaves. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which contribute to its ability to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing menstrual cramps, soothing digestive issues, and aiding in the healing of wounds. In herbal preparations, Agrimonia pubescens var. microcarpa can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
